name: Fix Next.js client/server component error with framer-motion in LoginPage
goal: Resolve the runtime error caused by using framer-motion's motion.div in a Next.js server component by ensuring the LoginPage is a client component.
summary: The error occurs because framer-motion (and its createMotionComponent) can only run on the client, but LoginPage is currently a server component. The fix is to mark LoginPage as a client component by adding 'use client' at the top of the file.
context_assumptions: framer-motion is only compatible with client components.
No other server-only logic is present in LoginPage.

files_to_modify.purpose: Mark LoginPage as a client component so framer-motion can be used without runtime errors.

constraints: Do not remove or refactor existing logic.
Only add 'use client' directive as needed.
Do not change the structure of the component.
acceptance_criteria: No runtime error about createMotionComponent being called from the server.
LoginPage renders with framer-motion animations as before.
notes: This is a standard Next.js app directory issue when using client-only libraries like framer-motion. The fix is minimal and safe.
